author: lOrm
content:
Resolved. Changes to `src/vdd/static_analysis.rs`:

**`run_chainlink_create`** — replaced all three `sh -c` invocations with direct `tokio::process::Command::new("chainlink")` + `.arg(title)` / `.arg(label)` / `.arg(comment)` argv dispatch. Backticks, `$()`, `;`, `|`, and newlines in `title`/`label`/`comment` are now inert because no shell is involved. Deleted the `.replace('"', '\\"')` pseudo-sanitization — unnecessary and misleading.

**`run_shell_command`** — parses the command string with POSIX `shlex` into argv tokens, then dispatches the same way: `Command::new(argv[0]).args(&argv[1..])`. Pipelines, redirections, and `&&`/`||` are no longer supported in this entry point by design — callers needing them must compose subprocess chains at the Rust level. This intentional capability loss is a security trade: the previous `sh -c` path meant any config-sourced command was an RCE surface.

**Ordering of newline stripping:** kept the `.replace('\\n', ' ')` on comment bodies, but now as UI courtesy (collapse for single-line display in the crosslink TUI) rather than quoting band-aid — documented with a code comment.

**Tests:** 6 new unit tests in `tools::chainlink::tests` exercise the argv machinery (rejects non-allowlisted subcommand, newline in token, unbalanced quotes, empty args, quoted multi-word arg, and injection via semicolons). All 595 lib tests pass.

**Out of scope / follow-up:**
- Structured `Result<Value, McpError>`-style error returns (mandated point 5 of #265) — would require touching every tool's return type; tracked separately as #376 (stringly-typed `(String, bool)` across every tool).
- Chainlink is invoked by name; if installed under a non-standard path, callers need to set PATH. No change from before.
